settings.text: Technicians experienced with Stockholm's buildings and housing types.
We handle local building permits, drilling permits and housing association approvals.
Fast service and spare parts throughout the Stockholm area.
Clear quote with no hidden costs – ROT deducted directly on the invoice.
Flexible financing via Klarna, instalments up to 36 months.

settings.question: How much does a heat pump cost in Stockholm?
settings.answer: A complete air-to-air installation starts from approx. 18,000 SEK. Ground source starts around 180,000 SEK including drilling. After the green deduction (gröna avdraget), which covers 50% of labour costs, the net cost is significantly lower. We provide a fixed quote after a free home visit.

settings.question: Does VARMA Energy install ground source heat pumps in Stockholm?
settings.answer: Yes. Ground source is the most common choice in Stockholm due to the area's older villa stock and larger plot sizes. Our technicians handle the entire process including drilling permits and final commissioning.

settings.question: Which heat pump suits my home?
settings.answer: For Stockholm's 1930s–1970s villas with water-based heating, ground source or air-to-water typically delivers the highest savings. For homes without a water system, air-to-air is the quickest and most affordable option. Our technician recommends the right type during the free home visit.

settings.question: How much can I save with a heat pump?
settings.answer: Most customers in Stockholm reduce annual heating costs by 50–65%. A typical villa saves around 34,500 SEK per year after switching from direct electric heating to a ground source heat pump.

settings.question: Do I get ROT deduction for heat pump installation?
settings.answer: Yes. The green deduction (gröna avdraget) covers 50% of the labour cost, up to 50,000 SEK per person per year. We deduct this directly on the invoice so you never pay upfront and apply later.

settings.hero_description: From villas in Bromma to terraced houses in Nacka – we have installed heat pumps in all types of homes in Stockholm. Clear quote and fixed price with no hidden costs.

settings.services_sub: Most popular in Stockholm: Ground source heat pump.
settings.local_heading: What Stockholm's housing stock looks like
settings.local_description: Stockholm has a mix of villas from the 1930s–1970s in Bromma and Nacka, modern terraced houses in Täby, and apartments in the inner city. Ground source delivers the greatest savings over time, but air-to-water is a cost-effective alternative when the plot is small.
settings.case_project: Ground source heat pump Nibe F1145
settings.case_result: Villa from 1942 – reduced heating cost from 52,000 SEK to 14,200 SEK/yr
settings.case_quote: The team was professional from start to finish. They explained everything and the installation was completed in three days. Highly recommended!
settings.process_sub: Four simple steps from first contact to commissioned heat pump.
settings.maint_description: Regular service keeps the pump efficient, extends its lifespan and preserves the manufacturer's warranty.
